title: Theme definitions
description: A theme documents an alternative set of token values — a color mode, density setting, or brand variant. DSDS captures its purpose, docs, and relationships; the actual override values live in the DTCG file `source` points to. Put internal links in `links` metadata, not a block.

tokenOverride.description: One token override in a theme: which token changes, and why. The value itself lives in the DTCG file — this just records the intent.

token.description: The token being overridden. MUST match a token's `identifier` defined elsewhere in the system. Token identifiers have no fixed pattern, to support DTCG and design-tool naming styles (dots, dashes, slashes).

description.description: What changes for this token in this theme, and why (ex: 'Lightened to meet WCAG AAA contrast on the dark background'). The resolved value lives in the DTCG file.

theme.description: A named context that adapts the system's token values — a color mode (light, dark, high-contrast), a density (compact, comfortable), a brand variant, or a product-specific tweak. DSDS captures the theme's purpose, docs, accessibility notes, and which tokens it affects; the override values live in the DTCG file `source` points to. The token catalog is still the source of truth for token identity, type, and docs.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a theme.

name.description: Display name for the theme (ex: 'Dark Mode', 'High Contrast', 'Compact Density').

description.description: What this theme is for, the context it adapts the system to, and when to apply it. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata: the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json).

source.description: Points to the DTCG (W3C Design Tokens) file. DSDS doesn't copy token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this theme's token override definitions.

path.description: The dot-path to this theme's group within the DTCG file, if applicable.

overrides.description: The token overrides this theme applies. List only tokens that differ from the default — anything unlisted keeps its default. The values themselves live in the DTCG file `source` points to.

relationships.description: Links from this theme to other entities — e.g. 'depends-on' the tokens it overrides, 'extends' a base theme. Tools derive the reverse edges.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this theme, in order. Themes accept the general kinds only (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist). Tools SHOULD keep this order for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Docs for agents (AI/LLM) only, using the same block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Put anything a human needs in `documentBlocks` — that's the default. Use this only for things that would be noise to a person: hard must/must-not rules, notes that correct a mistake agents commonly make (like picking a similar but wrong entity), evidence-backed constraints, and machine-checkable criteria. A rule humans need too still belongs in `documentBlocks`. This adds to the human docs, it never replaces them. Tools MUST NOT show these blocks to humans. Agents SHOULD read both arrays, human docs first.
